hero.question: Will Google have the best AI model at the end of March 2026?
hero.prob_take: The collapse from 10% to 5.5% in 24 hours signals serious money fleeing Google's chances, likely spooked by Claude 3.5's recent capabilities gains and OpenAI's o1 momentum eating into Gemini's competitive standing. At 5.5%, the market is overcorrecting on recent model releases and ignoring Google's infrastructure advantages and closed-loop advantage in search, but the bar for "best" is brutally high and OpenAI has narrative control heading into March 2026.

daily_take.headline: Google's AI Crown Slips to 5.5%. Smart Money Sees Bloodbath Coming.
daily_take.deck: Google dropped 4.5 points in 24 hours as the market reprices away the Gemini hype. At 5.5%, this is basically saying only 1 in 18 chance Google has the best model by March 2026. That's not just pessimism, it's capitulation. OpenAI's o1, Anthropic's Claude, even frontier Chinese labs are circling. The real question: has Google fallen this far already, or are we pricing in a total collapse? At these odds, you're getting paid to bet on a resurrection. Sharp money is asking if 5.5% is a gift or a trap.

sidebar.headline: Netanyahu's coalition math just got uglier. Down 5.5 points in one day means the market smells weakness nobody's reported yet.
sidebar.label: Netanyahu Out: 40.5%
